task_metadata.current_task: Implement delete operation with three cases: leaf nodes, nodes with one child, and nodes with two children

nodes.description: Define the Node class structure for the BST with value, left, and right pointers
nodes.rationale: A binary search tree fundamentally requires node objects to store values and maintain left/right child references. This is the foundation for insert, search, and delete operations.
nodes.task_state: Starting BST implementation - defining core data structure

nodes.description: Implement insert operation with recursive comparison and placement logic
nodes.rationale: Insert must maintain the BST property: values less than the node go left, greater or equal go right. Recursive approach is natural for tree traversal and is easier to implement correctly.

nodes.description: Implement search operation with recursive traversal and comparison
nodes.rationale: Search leverages the BST property for efficient O(log n) average case lookup. Recursive traversal comparing target value with current node determines whether to go left or right.

nodes.description: Implement delete operation with three cases: leaf nodes, nodes with one child, and nodes with two children
nodes.rationale: Delete requires handling three distinct cases: (1) leaf nodes simply remove, (2) nodes with one child replace node with that child, (3) nodes with two children find inorder successor (leftmost node in right subtree) and replace value, then recursively delete the successor

edges.rationale: Insert operation depends on Node structure being defined to create and link nodes during insertion
Search operation depends on Node structure to traverse and access node values and child pointers
